title: Moroccan Egg Seffa
description: A traditional Moroccan dish made by cooking eggs on a metal seffa (a type of tava griddle) with pickled vegetables, creating a jammy and snappy texture that kids love.
instructions: Heat a non-stick tava griddle or a metal seffa over medium heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
Add a small amount of olive oil to the hot pan and swirl it around.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the whisked eggs and let them cook for about 30 seconds.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the egg mixture towards the center.
Continue cooking and folding the eggs until they are almost set, about 2-3 minutes.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley and pickle slices over the top of the eggs.
Use your spatula to gently fold the eggs over the pickles and parsley.
Cook for another minute, until the eggs are fully set and the pickles are heated through.
